description: A constitutional referendum in Croatia held on December 1, 2013, to define marriage as a union between a man and a woman in the Constitution. The initiative was spearheaded by the 'In the Name of the Family' group and passed with 65.87% of the vote.

key_events.description: Petition signatures hit the threshold required for a mandatory referendum.
Croatian Constitutional Court rules the referendum is not unconstitutional.
The Catholic Church in Croatia formally urges the faithful to support the initiative.
Final TV debates show sharp polarization between progressive NGOs and religious groups.

calibration_notes: Shows influence of religious advocacy and judicial clearance on a social constitutional issue.
